title: trifolium sativum
intro: Trifolium sativum, also known as red clover, is a perennial legume plant native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, characterized by its pink or purple flowers and trifoliate leaves. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and estrogenic effects. The health benefits of red clover include its potential to alleviate menopausal symptoms, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function. Red clover is commonly prepared as an herbal tea, capsule, or tincture, and is also used as a nutritious feed supplement for livestock, particularly dairy cows and horses.
